title: Gaza aid flotilla vessels taken to Crete after Israeli naval interception
summary: Israeli naval forces intercepted the Global Sumud Flotilla carrying humanitarian aid bound for Gaza, seizing 22 vessels and detaining approximately 211 activists in international waters. The activists disembarked in Crete, Greece, after the interception.
contents:
## Israeli Navy Intercepts Gaza-Bound Aid Flotilla

### Incident
Israeli naval forces intercepted the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) in international waters near Crete, seizing more than 20 vessels carrying humanitarian aid destined for Gaza.

### Key Details
- Israel's foreign ministry reported approximately 175 activists detained from over 20 boats
- GSF organizers placed the number at 211 activists across 22 vessels
- Activists disembarked on the Greek island of Crete on Friday
- The interception occurred in international waters, prompting legal challenges

### Reactions
- GSF organizers condemned the interception as "piracy" and described their members as "abducted"
- The flotilla aimed to deliver humanitarian supplies to Gaza amid ongoing conflict
- International maritime law experts debated the legality of the interception in international waters
- Multiple nations called for an investigation into the incident

### Humanitarian Context
The interception occurred against the backdrop of ongoing Israeli military operations in Gaza and a deteriorating humanitarian situation. Aid organizations have repeatedly expressed concern about restricted access to civilian populations.

### Source Attribution
Al Jazeera and AFP reporting via Yahoo News World section.
